headline: Tag-verify gate now fetches `origin/main` before walking first-parent
summary: The integrity gate added in v1.11.7 walks `git rev-list --first-parent origin/main` to confirm a tagged commit landed on the main branch. On a tag-push event, `actions/checkout@v6` checks out the tag's commit and leaves `origin/main` either unset or stale, so the rev-list grep returned no match and the gate failed every release with a misleading 'not on main' error. The fix adds an explicit `git fetch --no-tags origin main:refs/remotes/origin/main` before the walk.

items.title: `sha-to-tag-verify.yml` — explicit `origin/main` fetch
items.body: The workflow now refreshes the `origin/main` remote-tracking ref before the first-parent walk, so the rev-list reflects the actual current state of the branch the tag was pushed against. The job's step name was also updated to drop the stale 'merged via PR' wording (the PR-merge check was removed in v1.11.8).
